blocks.title: Entry criteria
blocks.content: Adult with ovarian cyst/mass identified clinically or on ultrasound; symptoms may be incidental, chronic, or acute.

blocks.title: Emergency same-day
rules: IF acute severe unilateral pelvic pain +/- nausea/vomiting and torsion/rupture suspected THEN same-day emergency gynaecology assessment.

blocks.title: USC / suspected cancer referral
rules: IF ascites or pelvic/abdominal mass not clearly benign uterine/GI/urological origin THEN USC referral.
IF CA125 >=35 IU/ml with concerning symptoms/findings THEN USC referral.
IF ultrasound suspicious for malignancy THEN USC referral.

blocks.title: Premenopausal cyst thresholds
rules: IF simple cyst <5 cm THEN no routine follow-up.
IF simple cyst 5-7 cm THEN interval TVUS and refer if persistent or increasing.
IF simple cyst >7 cm THEN refer gynaecology.
IF non-simple/complex mass THEN CA125 (+LDH/AFP/hCG if age <40) and refer by risk.

blocks.title: Postmenopausal cyst thresholds
rules: IF simple unilateral unilocular cyst <=3 cm THEN no routine follow-up.
IF simple cyst 3-5 cm and CA125 <35 THEN repeat TVUS + CA125 every 6 months up to 1 year.
IF simple cyst >5 cm THEN urgent gynaecology referral.
IF any non-simple/indeterminate cyst/mass THEN refer with CA125 and RMI.

blocks.title: RMI escalation note
blocks.content: Local pathways commonly use RMI >200 for high-risk escalation; NICE specialist recommendation cites RMI >=250. Follow local USC/MDT escalation routes when high-risk threshold is reached.

blocks.title: Safety netting
rules: IF tests are non-diagnostic but symptoms persist/frequency increases THEN reassess promptly and repeat/escalate investigations.
Do not use a normal CA125 alone to exclude ovarian cancer.
